title: Boat Rental in Estepona Port: Costs and Tips for 2026
html:
Estepona Port offers a gateway to the Costa del Sol's sparkling waters, with boat hire Estepona Port starting from around €200 for a half-day on a small motorboat. Whether you're a seasoned skipper or a first-timer, BoatHire24 connects you directly with verified local boat owners.

Where to set off in Estepona Port

Depart from Puerto Deportivo de Estepona, the main marina with easy parking and fuel docks. Head west to Cabo de la Plata or east to the famous La Duquesa Marina. For a longer trip, set course for Gibraltar Bay or the serene Sotogrande Marina. The anchorage at Guadalmina Beach is a popular lunch stop. You can boat hire Estepona Port for your chosen dates.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Estepona Port?

Prices start at €180 for a shared tour and rise to €1,500 for a full-day crewed yacht. A half-day self-drive motorboat for up to 6 people typically costs €250 to €400. The primary link to compare verified boat listings is your best resource for accurate, up-to-date pricing.

  • Shared tours: from €60 per person, 2-3 hours
  • Small motorboat half-day: €200-€350, up to 6 guests
  • Catamaran full-day: €500-€900, up to 12 guests
  • Crewed yacht full-day: €1,200-€1,500, includes skipper and fuel

Which boat to rent

Your choice depends on group size and desired experience. Browse options on BoatHire24 to see real availability. For a stable family day out, consider a catamaran like a Lagoon 40. For speed, an Axopar 22 or Olympic 490 is hard to beat. A luxury yacht such as a Sunseeker or Astondoa turns a day trip into an event. One option worth a look is the Mefasa 90 listing.

  1. Motorboat, best for a quick coastal explore; seats 6; from €200 half-day
  2. Catamaran, stable and spacious; seats 12; from €500 full-day
  3. RIB, fast and nimble; seats 8; from €300 half-day
  4. Crewed yacht, luxury with crew; seats 10; from €1,200 full-day
  5. Fishing boat, equipped for angling; seats 4; from €250 half-day

For a stylish option, the Mefasa 90 listing on BoatHire24 offers a 9-metre motor yacht perfect for groups up to 6, complete with bimini and Bluetooth sound system.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Estepona Port?

In Spain, you generally need a licence for boats over 6 metres or engines above 15 horsepower. For smaller vessels, many operators allow self-drive without a licence after a short briefing. Always check with the boat owner on BoatHire24, as requirements can vary. The operator confirms the exact requirements before booking.

Peak season runs from June to August, when the Levanter wind can create a lively chop in the strait. Book at least a week ahead for the best choice.

At a glance

  • Price band: €180, €1,500 per day
  • Peak months: June, July, August
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full-day
  • Guest capacity: 4 to 12
  • Currency: EUR
  • Distance: 15 nautical miles to Gibraltar

Questions about boat rental in Estepona Port

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Estepona Port?

Yes, many operators offer small motorboats under 6 metres that do not require a licence, allowing you to self-drive with a quick safety briefing.

What is the best time of year for a boating trip from Estepona?

Late spring and early autumn offer calm seas and pleasant temperatures, while July and August are hottest but busiest with a chance of Levanter winds.

How many people can a typical rental boat accommodate?

Most motorboats seat 4 to 8 people, while catamarans and yachts can carry up to 12 passengers comfortably for a day trip.

What should I bring on a boat rental in Estepona?

Sunscreen, a hat, and a light jacket are essential; the boat owner usually provides safety equipment, a cool box, and sometimes snorkelling gear.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Estepona Port or list your own. Whether you are a first-time renter or a seasoned owner, the platform makes booking and listing straightforward. For 2026, new listings and updated availability mean even more choice for your Costa del Sol adventure.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Estepona Port and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
